MantrcJvara,
a reputed writer on astrology. The distinction between these
two minor planets (Upagrahas) consists in the fact that the
methods for calculating their rising periods are quite different.
I have already explained the procedure for finding out the
presence of Gulika in the horoscopic chart.
Mandi's rising is calculated as follows :—As before find out
the duration in Ghatis etc. of the day or night, as the case may
be. If the duration of the day is of 30 Ghatis, Mandi would
rise at the end of 26 Ghatis on Sunday, of 22 Ghatis on Monday,
of 18 on Tuesday, of 14 on Wednesday, of 10 on Thursday, of
6 on Friday, and lastly of 2 Ghatis on Saturday. In the case of
night births the 5th from that of the particular week day will
have to be considered. If the duration of the day or night is
more or less than 30 Ghatis, the above Ghatis given for the
rising of Mandi will have to be altered proportionately. In our
30 sfj 26
example it would be y - - Ghatis. That comes to 26
